etymology.description: Depicts an ancestral shrine (享) -> ancestral rites or offerings -> extension to concepts of enjoyment
etymology.interpretation: The character 享 (xiǎng, "enjoy; offer") originally depicts an ancestral shrine, which was associated with the practices of offering and veneration. This concept naturally extends to the offering of enjoyment or benefits, hence the evolved meaning of 'enjoy'.

examples.chinese: 我们在庭院里享受下午茶(我們在庭院裡享受下午茶)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒmen zài tíngyuàn lǐ xiǎngshòu xiàwǔ chá.
examples.english: We enjoy afternoon tea in the courtyard.

examples.chinese: 他喜欢享受独自旅行的自由(他喜歡享受獨自旅行的自由)。
examples.pinyin: Tā xǐhuan xiǎngshòu dúzì lǚxíng de zìyóu.
examples.english: He enjoys the freedom of traveling alone.

examples.chinese: 爷爷总是在晚饭后享用他的碗茶(爺爺總是在晚飯後享用他的碗茶)。
examples.pinyin: Yéye zǒng shì zài wǎnfàn hòu xiǎngyòng tā de wǎn chá.
examples.english: Grandfather always enjoys his bowl of tea after dinner.
